lines: +config-entry(api-gateway): (Enterprise only) Add ExtAuthzFilter to HTTPRoute Filters and gateway-wide ExtAuthz toggle to the api-gateway config entry
// CanApply indicates if the ext-authz extension can be applied to the given extension runtime configuration.
// PatchClusters modifies the cluster resources for the ext-authz extension.
// PatchFilters inserts an ext-authz filter into the list of network filters or the filter chain of the HTTP connection manager.
func (a *extAuthz) PatchFilters(cfg *ext_cmn.RuntimeConfig, filters []*envoy_listener_v3.Filter, isInboundListener bool) ([]*envoy_listener_v3.Filter, error) {
+	// On an API Gateway the ext_authz filter is applied to the gateway's HTTP
+	// listeners. Gateway listeners are not the sidecar "public_listener" and so are
+	// not classified as inbound; we therefore bypass the inbound/outbound direction
+	// check that applies to sidecar proxies. This path is enterprise-only.
// The ext_authz extension only patches filters for inbound listeners.
+// patchAPIGatewayFilters inserts the ext_authz HTTP filter into the HTTP connection
+// manager of an API Gateway listener filter chain. Filter chains that are not managed
+// by an HTTP connection manager (e.g. TCPRoute listeners) are skipped without error.
+		// No HTTP connection manager on this filter chain; nothing to patch.
-	// The filter's failure mode is always configured based on whether or not the extension is required.
+	// By default the filter's failure mode derives from whether the extension is
+	// required (required => fail closed). An explicit Config.FailureModeAllow, if
+	// set, takes precedence and overrides that default.
+	// Propagate the resolved proxy kind so extAuthzConfig.validate() can apply
+	// kind-specific rules (e.g. URI host restrictions differ between sidecar and gateway).
-		resultErr = multierror.Append(resultErr, fmt.Errorf("unsupported ProxyType %q, only %q is supported",
+		// The set of supported proxy types depends on the build: API Gateway
+		resultErr = multierror.Append(resultErr, fmt.Errorf("unsupported ProxyType %q, supported values are %s",
+// apiGatewayExtAuthzSupported reports whether the builtin/ext-authz extension may
+// be applied to API Gateway proxies. This is an enterprise-only capability, so it
+// is always false in CE; the enterprise build overrides it to true.
+// TestAPIGatewayExtAuthzSupported_CE verifies that API Gateway support for the
+// builtin/ext-authz extension is disabled in CE. This is an enterprise-only
+// capability; the enterprise build overrides it to true.
+// extension with an api-gateway ProxyType is rejected in CE, and that the error
+// only advertises connect-proxy as a supported value.
+// cannot be applied to an API Gateway proxy in CE, while connect-proxy is still
+// renderExtAuthz builds the extension from arguments (with the given Required
+// flag) and returns the decoded Envoy ext_authz HTTP filter config.
+// grpcURIArgs builds connect-proxy ext-authz arguments with a localhost gRPC
+// URI target, merging any extra top-level Config keys.
+// TestExtAuthz_FailureModeAllow verifies that the explicit Config.FailureModeAllow
+// overrides the default derived from the extension's Required flag.
+	t.Run("override true wins over required=true", func(t *testing.T) {
+		require.True(t, got.GetFailureModeAllow(), "explicit Config.FailureModeAllow=true must override Required-derived default")
+	t.Run("override false wins over required=false", func(t *testing.T) {
+		require.False(t, got.GetFailureModeAllow(), "explicit Config.FailureModeAllow=false must override Required-derived default")
+// TestExtAuthz_ServiceTimeout verifies the service-level Timeout field on both
+// GrpcService and HttpService, including precedence over Target.Timeout.
+	t.Run("grpc service-level Timeout is used", func(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Run("grpc service-level Timeout takes precedence over Target.Timeout", func(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Run("grpc falls back to Target.Timeout when service Timeout unset", func(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Run("http service-level Timeout is used on server_uri", func(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Run("invalid grpc service Timeout is rejected", func(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Run("invalid http service Timeout is rejected", func(t *testing.T) {
+// AllowedHeaders / DisallowedHeaders controls are rendered onto the Envoy
+// ext_authz filter, and that they are omitted (nil) when unset.
+	t.Run("allowed and disallowed headers are rendered", func(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Run("invalid AllowedHeaders matcher is rejected", func(t *testing.T) {
+				// No match pattern set on the matcher -> validation error.
+	t.Run("invalid DisallowedHeaders matcher is rejected", func(t *testing.T) {
+				// More than one match pattern set -> validation error.
+// TestExtAuthz_ToEnvoyCluster verifies the local ext_authz cluster generated for
+// a URI target: STRICT_DNS is used for DNS names (including "localhost") and
+// STATIC for bare IP addresses, and the connect timeout reflects the effective
+	t.Run("localhost DNS name uses STRICT_DNS", func(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Run("connect timeout reflects service Timeout", func(t *testing.T) {
+// sidecar rejects a non-loopback URI target host, and that the error points the
+// user at the api-gateway proxy type for remote hosts.
+	t.Run("remote host rejected for connect-proxy", func(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Run("loopback host accepted for connect-proxy", func(t *testing.T) {
+			require.NoError(t, err, "host %q should be accepted", host)
+	// FailureModeAllow, when set, explicitly controls the ext_authz filter's
+	// failure-mode behavior and overrides the default that is otherwise derived
+	//   - true  -> requests are ALLOWED when the authorization service is
+	//              unreachable or returns an error (fail open).
+	//   - false -> such requests are DENIED (fail closed).
+	// When nil (omitted) the Required-derived default is used.
+	// AllowedHeaders restricts which client request headers are forwarded to the
+	// external authorization service in the check request. It maps to the
+	// filter-level Envoy field ext_authz.allowed_headers and therefore applies to
+	// BOTH the gRPC and HTTP transports.
+	// When unset, Envoy keeps its default behavior: ALL client request headers are
+	// sent to a gRPC authorization server, whereas only a small default set is sent
+	// to an HTTP authorization server (plus any HttpService.AuthorizationRequest
+	// .AllowedHeaders). This field is the only way to restrict the request headers
+	// sent to a gRPC authorization server.
+	// DisallowedHeaders explicitly prevents the listed client request headers from
+	// being forwarded to the external authorization service. It maps to the
+	// filter-level Envoy field ext_authz.disallowed_headers and takes precedence
+	// over AllowedHeaders when a header matches both.
+	// proxyKind is set by extAuthz.normalize() and controls validation rules that
+	// differ between proxy kinds (e.g. URI host restrictions).
resultErr = multierror.Append(resultErr, fmt.Errorf("failed to validate Config.%s: %w", field, err))
+	// For connect-proxy sidecars the ext_authz backend must be on the local host
+	// because the sidecar and the authz service share the same network namespace.
+	// For API Gateway proxies the backend can be any network-reachable host (e.g. a
+	// separate container), so the localhost restriction is not applied.
+					fmt.Errorf("invalid host for Target.URI %q: expected %q, %q, or %q (use ProxyType %q to allow remote hosts)",
resultErr = multierror.Append(resultErr, fmt.Errorf("failed to validate Config.StatusOnError:"+
+		resultErr = multierror.Append(resultErr, fmt.Errorf("failed to validate Config.AllowedHeaders: %w", err))
+		resultErr = multierror.Append(resultErr, fmt.Errorf("failed to validate Config.DisallowedHeaders: %w", err))
Uri:              clusterName, // not used by Envoy, set to cluster
// a new cluster so this method returns nil.
// If the target is an upstream we do not need to create a cluster. We will use the cluster of the upstream.
-		// If the host is "localhost" use a STRICT_DNS cluster type to perform DNS lookup.
+		// host is a DNS name (including "localhost" and container names like "oauth2-proxy").
+		// Use STRICT_DNS so Envoy resolves it at runtime instead of treating it as a
+		// literal IP. STATIC is used only for bare IP addresses.
+	// AllowedHeaders / DisallowedHeaders are filter-level controls that apply to
+	// both the gRPC and HTTP transports. toEnvoy() returns nil for an empty list,
+	// preserving Envoy's default header-forwarding behavior when unset.
+	// Timeout sets the per-request timeout for calls to the gRPC authorization
+	// service, expressed as a Go duration string (e.g. "2s"). When set it takes
+	// precedence over Target.Timeout; when empty the Target.Timeout is used.
+		if d, err := time.ParseDuration(v.Timeout); err == nil {
+			resultErr = multierror.Append(resultErr, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse GrpcService.Timeout %q as a duration: %w", v.Timeout, err))
+// timeoutDurationPB returns the effective per-request timeout as a
+// *durationpb.Duration. The service-level Timeout takes precedence over
+// Target.Timeout; nil is returned when neither is set.
+	// Timeout sets the per-request timeout for calls to the HTTP authorization
+	// service, expressed as a Go duration string (e.g. "2s"). When set it takes
+	// precedence over Target.Timeout; when empty the Target.Timeout is used.
+		if d, err := time.ParseDuration(v.Timeout); err == nil {
+			resultErr = multierror.Append(resultErr, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse HttpService.Timeout %q as a duration: %w", v.Timeout, err))
+// timeoutDurationPB returns the effective per-request timeout as a
+// *durationpb.Duration. The service-level Timeout takes precedence over
+// Target.Timeout; nil is returned when neither is set.
-					fmt.Errorf("invalid host for Target.URI %q: expected %q, %q, or %q", t.URI, localhost, localhostIPv4, localhostIPv6))
resultErr = multierror.Append(resultErr, fmt.Errorf("invalid format for Target.URI %q: expected host:port", t.URI))
+		// Localhost-only enforcement for connect-proxy is done in extAuthzConfig.validate()
+		// where the proxy kind is available. Target.validate() only checks format here.
